executive_summary: Idle Kingdom Revolution uses a modern JAMstack architecture with serverless backend, edge deployment, and real-time data synchronization. The stack prioritizes developer velocity, cost efficiency, and scalability.

frontend.state_management: Zustand 5.x (game state, persist middleware) + TanStack Query 5.x (installed, not yet integrated)
frontend.why: Next.js provides SSR/SSG for SEO, API routes for backend logic, and excellent PWA support. App Router is the modern standard.

backend.edge_functions: Supabase Edge Functions (Deno/TypeScript) for heavy compute
backend.api_layer: Next.js API Routes (for game logic that needs to be close to frontend)
backend.why_supabase: Open source, PostgreSQL-based, generous free tier, realtime subscriptions, row-level security, no vendor lock-in like Firebase

database.caching: Redis (Upstash) for hot data + rate limiting
database.why: PostgreSQL is battle-tested, supports JSONB for flexible game data, has excellent GIS support for map features

storage.cdn: Cloudflare R2 or Supabase CDN (free tier sufficient for start)

deployment.frontend: Netlify (free tier, automatic deploys from Git)

testing.coverage_target: >80% for business logic, >60% for UI components

devops.git_strategy: Git Flow (main, develop, feature/*, release/*, hotfix/*)

data_flow.sync_strategy: Optimistic UI with rollback on conflict
data_flow.conflict_resolution: Server timestamp wins, client gets notification
data_flow.queue_mechanism: Offline actions queued in IndexedDB, synced when online

security.auth: Supabase Auth (JWT tokens, refresh token rotation)
security.rls: Row Level Security on ALL database tables
security.admin_access: Separate admin role with elevated RLS policies

scalability.current_plan: Free tiers across all services (sufficient for ~10k MAU)

firebase.verdict: REJECTED - vendor lock-in and query limitations

cons: Less generous free tier than Netlify for bandwidth
vercel.verdict: ALTERNATIVE - use if Netlify limits hit

cons: QNAP NAS too weak for production
self_hosted.verdict: REJECTED for MVP - reconsider for v2.0 if game succeeds

aws_amplify.verdict: REJECTED - too complex for indie project

supabase_dependency.risk: Medium - if Supabase changes pricing or shuts down
supabase_dependency.mitigation: PostgreSQL is standard - migration to any Postgres host is straightforward
supabase_dependency.backup_strategy: Daily pg_dump to S3/R2, schema always in version control

netlify_limits.risk: Low - free tier is generous for static sites
netlify_limits.mitigation: Easy migration to Vercel or Cloudflare Pages

realtime_performance.risk: Medium - WebSocket connections can be flaky on mobile
realtime_performance.mitigation: Graceful degradation to polling, local state as source of truth

state_management_note: Zustand integrated into game layout for initial data population (profile, units, formation, inventory). Army page reads from store. TanStack Query installed but not yet active -- manual fetch pattern used. Zustand persist middleware saves to localStorage for cross-page cache.
